name: scarlet-ui
description: Live native preview for ScarletUI crates via the scarlet-ui-preview IPC server.

scarlet-ui.serverCommand.markdownDescription: Command array to start the ScarletUI preview server. The extension appends `--manifest-path <targetManifest>` to this command. Default is an installed binary: `scarlet-ui-preview serve`. Install it with `cargo install --path tools/preview` (run from the scarlet-ui repo). Important: even with the installed binary, preview builds still shell out to `cargo`/`rustc`, so those must also be available on PATH; on macOS, the extension also uses `rustc --print target-libdir` to mirror Cargo's dynamic library lookup for installed `scarlet-ui-preview` launches. For Nix users, launch VS Code from `nix develop` or set a wrapper command. For dev without installing, use `["cargo", "run", "--manifest-path", "tools/preview/Cargo.toml", "--", "serve"]`.

scarlet-ui.targetManifest.markdownDescription: Path to the `Cargo.toml` of the crate to preview. If `null`, the workspace root `Cargo.toml` is used (must be a package, not a virtual workspace).

scarlet-ui.autoFollowActiveEditor.markdownDescription: When enabled, switching the active Rust editor auto-switches the preview source via `preview/switchSource` for files that define `#[scarlet_ui::preview]` functions.
